needs_human_review.message: In TryGetValidatableTypeInfo, the out parameter validatableTypeInfo is now assigned null before the loop, but the loop may assign a non-null value via resolver.TryGetValidatableTypeInfo. If the loop completes without finding a match, the out parameter will be null (as before). However, the assignment at line 42 is redundant and could mask a bug if a resolver incorrectly returns true without setting the out parameter, because the out parameter would already be null from the initial assignment, making the [NotNullWhen(true)] contract unreliable.
needs_human_review.suggestion: Remove the redundant 'validatableTypeInfo = null;' assignment at line 42, as the out parameter is already assigned by the resolver or defaults to null at the end of the method. Alternatively, ensure that each resolver correctly sets the out parameter when returning true.

The primary evidence for ValidateContext.cs only shows a documentation change. The omitted hunks may contain behavioral changes that introduce risks.
The primary evidence only shows a minor documentation change. To assess whether the patch introduces any functional risk, the remaining diff hunks are needed.

needs_human_review.message: In TryGetValidatableTypeInfo, the out parameter validatableTypeInfo is now assigned null before the loop, but the loop may assign a non-null value via resolver.TryGetValidatableTypeInfo. If the loop completes without finding a match, the out parameter will be null (as before). However, the assignment at line 42 is redundant and could mask a bug if a resolver incorrectly returns true without setting the out parameter, because the out parameter would already be null from the initial assignment, making the [NotNullWhen(true)] contract unreliable.
needs_human_review.suggestion: Remove the redundant 'validatableTypeInfo = null;' assignment at line 42, as the out parameter is already assigned by the resolver or defaults to null at the end of the method. Alternatively, ensure that each resolver correctly sets the out parameter when returning true.
